summary: DHH argues that original thinking requires deliberately reducing exposure to the constant noise of industry trends and social media. He credits 37signals' innovative work in product development and organizational design to being physically and mentally distant from Silicon Valley. He contrasts the pre-social-media era, when it was easier to tune out hype, with today's environment where every feed pushes you into predefined camps. The core message is that less exposure to conventional wisdom can paradoxically raise the bar for what you can achieve.
key_points: Original thinking is impossible when your senses are perpetually flooded with everyone else's ideas and established scripts
37signals' geographic and cultural distance from Silicon Valley was a key factor in their novel approaches to product and organizational design
Social media has made it exponentially harder to escape industry groupthink compared to the pre-social-media era
Taking sides for or against a trend (like AI) is still letting that trend dictate your thinking — both positions are 'plusses and minuses in front of the same thinking'
Sometimes knowing less about what everyone else is doing raises the ceiling on what you can create
money_quotes: It's hard to think original thoughts if your senses are being perpetually flooded with everything from everyone all the time.
To escape back to first principles, you need to consciously turn down the volume on the world.
Being in Chicago in those early years of the post-dot-com-bust internet was like living in a monastery.
Every feed is there to homogenize your thinking into a preconfigured camp. For or against, hype or denounce. These are all just plusses and minuses in front of the same thinking.
There are times when the less you know raises the bar for what you can do.
Don't drown out our inner drumbeat by living all your days inside the permanent intellectual festival of your profession.
key_insight: Deliberately distancing yourself from industry noise and conventional wisdom is not ignorance — it's a prerequisite for original thinking.
